sources.name: UKCEH Inventory of Reservoirs (273 reservoirs, ~90% of UK storage)

constructionByDecade.description: Number of major reservoirs constructed per decade and capacity added by use type, from UKCEH inventory. Hydro-electric capacity estimated from identified large Scottish hydro schemes.

cumulativeCapacity.description: Cumulative reservoir count and storage capacity by decade and use type, with UK population and per-capita storage. Hydro-electric share estimated from identified large schemes.
cumulativeCapacity.unit: count / billion litres / litres per person

largest.description: Ten largest UK reservoirs by storage capacity

planned.description: Major reservoirs currently planned or under construction in England, from RAPID and water company announcements

data.name: Havant Thicket
data.costNote: Up from Ofwat's original PR19 allowance of £124m (2017-18 prices). Ofwat adjusted price control to £370m (Nov 2021 prices).
data.statusDetail: Main dam and embankment works underway. 13m steel cut-off wall installed February 2026. First major reservoir in England since the early 1990s.

data.name: SESRO (Abingdon)
data.developer: Thames Water / Southern Water / Affinity Water
data.costNote: Main works advertised at £5.7bn (Q2 2025 prices, excl. inflation). Government range £5.5-7.5bn. Thames Water cited up to £7.5bn in August 2025. Originally ~£2.2bn at early market engagement.
data.statusDetail: DCO application targeted Autumn 2026. Statutory consultation held 2025. Judicial review dismissed July 2025. Would be second-largest reservoir in England after Kielder Water (670 ha surface area).

data.name: Fens Reservoir
data.costNote: Capital cost at 2022-23 prices. Whole-life total expenditure estimated at nearly £6bn.
data.statusDetail: Designated as Nationally Significant Infrastructure Project (NSIP) in spring 2025. DCO application slipped from 2026 to early 2027. Cost-saving design update revealed October 2025.

data.name: Lincolnshire Reservoir
data.costNote: Combined cost with Fens Reservoir: £4.8bn. Main works for both advertised at £5bn.
data.statusDetail: Designated as NSIP in spring 2025. DCO application pushed back ~2 years to late 2028 / early 2029. Phase two consultations planned 2026-2027.

data.name: Cheddar 2
data.location: Adjacent to existing Cheddar Reservoir, Somerset
data.costNote: Capital cost excluding VAT. Total expenditure including operations/maintenance ~£1bn. Government committed £84m in direct funding. Includes 75 km potable water pipeline from Bristol to Devon.
data.statusDetail: Planning application targeted 2027. Contract award May 2029. Construction 2029-2034. Project was scrapped in 2018 and revived after Bristol Water merger in 2023.

data.name: Broad Oak
data.location: North of Broad Oak, near Canterbury, Kent
data.costNote: Ground investigation works completed July-November 2025. RAPID Gate Two milestone pushed from December 2025 to September 2026.
data.statusDetail: Ground investigation phase. Public consultation February-March 2026. Planning application submission planned early 2028, construction start ~2030.

projectedCapacity.description: Projected total reservoir capacity and per-capita storage 2020-2040, assuming planned reservoirs come online at expected dates. Population from ONS 2022-based principal projection.

sesroTimeline.description: Key milestones in the SESRO (Abingdon Reservoir) project, from first proposal in 2006 to projected completion in 2040

data.event: Thames Water identifies Upper Thames Reservoir in draft WRMP
Public inquiry into Thames Water's WRMP including reservoir proposal
Secretary of State rejects the Abingdon reservoir after Planning Inspector recommends against
National Infrastructure Commission recommends new strategic water resources for the South East
RAPID (Regulators' Alliance) formed; SESRO enters gated assessment process
Thames Water includes SESRO in revised WRMP; scheme formally revived
Statutory consultation held; judicial review dismissed (July); cost estimate £5.7bn

reservoirLocations.label: UK Reservoir Locations
reservoirLocations.description: Geographic coordinates for UK reservoirs from Wikidata, with capacity data from UKCEH inventory where available. Covers 978 reservoirs.
